title: Finite-dimensional block-centralizer linear product of block-fiber type-I factors
summary: Jones-local supporting theorem card for the current finite-dimensional route: the block centralizer is identified with a linear product of block-fiber full-operator or type-I pieces inside the bounded Chapter 4 packet.
assumptions: The current theorem remains bounded to the finite-dimensional Chapter 4 packet and does not claim the stronger algebra-level product equivalence.

approximations: linear finite-product theorem rather than stronger algebra equivalence
boundary_conditions: the theorem is used as supporting structure for the current Jones finite-product packet
exclusions: the stronger subalgebra-level AlgEquiv product theorem

applies_to: the bounded finite-dimensional block-centralizer product theorem used by the current Jones route
out_of_scope: later Lane A/B/C routes beyond the bounded packet

promotion.rationale: Promote the supporting linear product statement as a standalone Jones theorem card so the corpus can carry one theorem neighborhood instead of one isolated packet.

payload.statement: Within the bounded Jones finite-dimensional Chapter 4 route, the block centralizer is packaged as a linear product of block-fiber full-operator or type-I pieces.
